name: Flare Lab: high request rate by source IP
description: Signal when one source IP produces 100 or more access events. Lab threshold only; validate with owned traffic.
false_positives: Load tests and shared NATs can be noisy.

name: Flare Lab: repeated 403/404 by source IP
description: Signal when one source IP causes 20 or more 403/404 responses during the lookback.
false_positives: Broken links and browser prefetches can be noisy.

name: Flare Lab: authentication brute-force signal
description: Signal when a source IP receives five login 401/429 responses. It uses the existing application rate limiter; it does not alter authentication behavior.
query: http.request.method: POST and url.path: "/api/auth/login" and http.response.status_code: (401 or 429)
false_positives: A user repeatedly entering an incorrect password.

name: Flare Lab: repeated server errors
description: Signal when five 5xx responses occur. Validate with the supplied local fixture, never by adding a failure endpoint to the application.

name: Flare Lab: SQLi/XSS/traversal heuristic
description: Creates an alert for one high severity URL heuristic. A match is a triage signal, not proof of exploitation.
false_positives: Security testing and encoded examples in documentation.

name: Flare Lab: scanner User-Agent heuristic
description: Creates an alert for one scanner User-Agent heuristic. A match is a triage signal, not proof of malicious intent.
